key_insight: ReNIO improves on-policy distillation by upweighting informative negative student trajectories using prefix-only token probability ratios, instead of treating all self-generated outputs equally.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to boost LLM reasoning and code generation while preserving the efficiency advantage of prefix-based training over full-rollout reinforcement learning.
method: Compute the student-to-teacher probability ratio over prefixes to detect pivotal tokens in wrong reasoning traces.
Aggregate token-level signals into a normalized sample weight that favors likely negative trajectories.
Use these weights to train OPD/OPSD without needing final-answer correctness labels.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning and code generation benchmarks, the method improves both OPD and OPSD, with reported gains up to 8.90% on Qwen3-1.7B and 10.00% on R1-Distill-Qwen-7B for math tasks.
watch_item: Because ReNIO relies on prefix-conditioned probabilities and inferred negativity, its effectiveness may depend on calibration quality and may be sensitive to noisy teacher-student probability estimates.
paper_title: ReNIO: Reweighting Negative Trajectory Importance for LLM On-Policy Distillation
